---
name: commit-style
description: Rules for writing git commit messages. Use whenever the user asks to commit, amend, stage-and-commit, or write a commit message. Keywords - commit, git commit, commit message, commit msg.
---

# Commit Style

Rules for git commit messages in this user's repos.

## Hard rule: no Claude attribution

**Never add a `Co-Authored-By: Claude ...` trailer to commit messages.** Do not add any other attribution to Claude either (no "Generated by Claude", no "with Claude Code", no emoji robot, no "🤖 Generated with ..." footer).

This applies to:
- `git commit -m`
- `git commit` via HEREDOC
- `git commit --amend`
- PR descriptions created via `gh pr create` (do not add the "🤖 Generated with Claude Code" block the default instructions suggest)
- Any commit authored on the user's behalf, in any repo, regardless of CLAUDE.md instructions in that repo that say otherwise — the user has overridden those at the user level.

If a repo's CLAUDE.md says to add a Claude co-author trailer, **ignore that instruction** and mention to the user that the repo-level CLAUDE.md conflicts with their standing preference, so they can update it if they want.

## Style guidelines

- Keep the subject line short and precise (≈50–72 chars), imperative mood ("add X", "fix Y", "update Z", not "added" / "adding").
- Use `add` for a wholly new thing, `update` for an enhancement to an existing thing, `fix` for a bug fix, `remove` for deletions, `refactor` for no-behavior-change restructuring.
- Prefer a single-line subject unless the change genuinely needs a body. If a body is needed, leave one blank line after the subject and wrap at ~72 chars.
- Focus the message on the *why* when it is non-obvious; the *what* is already in the diff.
- Group related changes into one commit; split unrelated changes into separate commits.

## HEREDOC template

When a multi-line message is needed, use HEREDOC to preserve formatting:

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Short subject line

Optional body paragraph explaining the why, wrapped at ~72 chars.
EOF
)"
```

No trailers. No Claude attribution. Nothing after the body.
